_note: This file is a design-time snapshot and may be out of date. For current step state, run: iw item-status <ID> --json. The DB is the authoritative source of truth (CR-00023).
title: Don't cascade merge-time failures to dependent items

steps.description: Add merge_failed value to BatchItemStatus enum; add Alembic migration with no-op downgrade; update TERMINAL_BATCH_ITEM_STATUSES to include merge_failed
Review S01 enum addition + migration: reversibility note, naming, terminal-set membership

merge_queue.py:289 → BatchItemStatus.merge_failed (line 136 stays failed); batch_manager.py:59 _BLOCKING_TERMINAL_STATUSES excludes {merged, merge_failed, migration_invalid, migration_rebase_failed}; batch_manager.py:1368 _current_execution_group treats those three as non-terminal; actions.py restart-merge accepts the three statuses; new abandon-merge endpoint flips merge_failed/migration_invalid/migration_rebase_failed → failed and emits merge_abandoned event
Review S03: invariant 'setup runs only after previous merge is 100% complete' is preserved; cascade still fires on legacy failed; restart-merge / abandon-merge preconditions are correct

Add merge_failed badge variant (distinct from failed); render Retry-merge and Abandon buttons on rows whose status is in {merge_failed, migration_invalid, migration_rebase_failed} on batch detail and item overview templates; htmx POST targets the existing/new endpoints
Review S05: badge accessibility (color contrast, aria-label), no-regression on existing badge variants, htmx POST target correctness

Unit tests for: _merge_item writes merge_failed (and still writes failed on no-worktree-path branch); _BLOCKING_TERMINAL_STATUSES membership; _current_execution_group treats merge_failed as non-terminal; restart-merge precondition; abandon-merge endpoint. Integration tests for: full 2-item-2-group cascade-not-triggered scenario; abandon-merge triggers cascade
Review S07 tests for coverage of all 7 acceptance criteria and isolation
Cross-step global review: enum → daemon → actions → templates → tests chain holds end-to-end; no orphan code paths still using old failed transition for recoverable merge errors
QV: Browser verification — merge_failed badge renders, Retry/Abandon buttons present and wired, no console errors, no regression on existing batch detail UI
